title: Type-Safe APIs — Let the Compiler Do the Talking
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Branded types and generic constraints eliminate entire categories of API misuse at compile time
content.proof: type LatticeCoord = number & { __brand: 'LatticeCoord' } creates a nominal type from TypeScript's structural system. The compiler prevents passing a raw number where a LatticeCoord is expected. Combined with generic constraints: function snap<C extends LatticeCoord>(coord: C): SnapResult<C> — the return type depends on the input type.
content.benchmark: Compile-time type checking: 0 runtime cost. Incorrect API usage caught: 100% of tested misuse patterns.
content.caveat: Branded types require assertion functions to create. Runtime validation (zod, io-ts) is still needed at API boundaries.
